children.heading: Enrollment
children.label: A Enrollment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a pilot program to enroll land in the conservation reserve program through a 30-year conservation reserve contract (referred to in this subsection as a “CLEAR 30 contract”) in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: Inclusion of acreage limitation
children.label: B Inclusion of acreage limitation
children.text: For purposes of applying the limitations in , the Secretary shall include acres of land enrolled under this subsection.section 3831(d)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: expires on or after ; andDecember 20, 2018

children.label: ii
children.text: covers land enrolled in the conservation reserve program under the clean lakes, estuaries, and rivers priority described in (or the predecessor practices that constitute the priority, as determined by the Secretary).section 3831(d)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: not to reenroll the land under the contract;

children.label: ii
children.text: to offer to reenroll the land under the contract if the land remains eligible under the terms in effect as of the date of expiration; or

children.label: iii
children.text: not to reenroll the land under the contract and to enroll that land through a CLEAR 30 contract under this subsection.

children.heading: Eligible land
children.label: 3 Eligible land
children.text: Only land that is subject to an expired covered contract shall be eligible for enrollment through a CLEAR 30 contract under this subsection.

children.heading: Term
children.label: 4 Term
children.text: The term of a CLEAR 30 contract shall be 30 years.

children.label: A
children.text: to implement a conservation reserve plan developed for the land;

children.label: B
children.text: to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract and any related agreements; and

children.label: C
children.text: to temporarily suspend the base history for the land covered by the contract.

children.heading: Terms and conditions of CLEAR 30 contracts
children.label: 6 Terms and conditions of CLEAR 30 contracts
I
children.text: repairs, improvements, and inspections on the land that are necessary to maintain existing public drainage systems; and

children.label: II
children.text: owners to control public access on the land while identifying access routes to be used for restoration activities and management and contract monitoring;

children.label: I
children.text: the alteration of wildlife habitat and other natural features of the land, unless specifically authorized by the Secretary as part of the conservation reserve plan;

children.label: aa
children.text: to comply with Federal or State noxious weed control laws;

children.label: bb
children.text: to comply with a Federal or State emergency pest treatment program; or

children.label: cc
children.text: to meet habitat needs of specific wildlife species;

children.label: III
children.text: any activity to be carried out on the land of the owner or successor that is immediately adjacent to, and functionally related to, the land that is subject to the contract if the activity will alter, degrade, or otherwise diminish the functional value of the land; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the adoption of any other practice that would tend to defeat the purposes of the conservation reserve program, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: include any additional provision that the Secretary determines is appropriate to carry out this section or facilitate the practical administration of this section.

children.heading: Violation
children.label: B Violation
children.text: On the violation of a term or condition of a CLEAR 30 contract, the Secretary may require the owner to refund all or part of any payments received by the owner under the conservation reserve program, with interest on the payments, as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: is specifically permitted by the conservation reserve plan developed for the land; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is consistent with the long-term protection and enhancement of the conservation resources for which the contract was established.

children.heading: Amount of payments
children.label: A Amount of payments
children.text: The Secretary shall provide payment under this subsection to an owner of land enrolled through a CLEAR 30 contract using 30 annual payments in an amount equal to the amount that would be used if the land were to be enrolled in the conservation reserve program under .section 3831(d)(3) of this title

children.heading: Form of payment
children.label: B Form of payment
children.text: Compensation for a CLEAR 30 contract shall be provided by the Secretary in the form of a cash payment in an amount determined under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Timing
children.label: C Timing
children.text: The Secretary shall provide any annual payment obligation under subparagraph (A) as early as practicable in each fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: dies;

children.label: ii
children.text: becomes incompetent;

children.label: iii
children.text: is succeeded by another person or entity who renders or completes the required performance; or

children.label: iv
children.text: is otherwise unable to receive the payment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall assist owners in complying with the terms and conditions of a CLEAR 30 contract.

children.heading: Contracts or agreements
children.label: B Contracts or agreements
children.text: The Secretary may enter into 1 or more contracts with private entities or agreements with a State, nongovernmental organization, or Indian Tribe to carry out necessary maintenance of a CLEAR 30 contract if the Secretary determines that the contract or agreement will advance the purposes of the conservation reserve program.

children.heading: Conservation reserve plan
children.label: A Conservation reserve plan
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a conservation reserve plan for any land subject to a CLEAR 30 contract, which shall include practices and activities necessary to maintain, protect, and enhance the conservation value of the enrolled land.

children.heading: Federal, State, or local government agencies
children.label: i Federal, State, or local government agencies
children.text: The Secretary may delegate any of the management, monitoring, and enforcement responsibilities of the Secretary under this subsection to other Federal, State, or local government agencies that have the appropriate authority, expertise, and resources necessary to carry out those delegated responsibilities.

children.heading: Conservation organizations
children.label: ii Conservation organizations
children.text: The Secretary may delegate any management responsibilities of the Secretary under this subsection to conservation organizations if the Secretary determines the conservation organization has similar expertise and resources.

children.heading: Soil health and income protection pilot program
children.label: b Soil health and income protection pilot program
i
children.text: is selected by the owner or operator of the land for proposed enrollment in the pilot program under this subsection; and

children.label: I
children.text: is located within 1 or more States that are part of the prairie pothole region, as selected by the Secretary based on consultation with State Committees of the Farm Service Agency and State technical committees established under from that region;section 3861(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: had a cropping history or was considered to be planted during each of the 3 crop years preceding enrollment; and

children.label: III
children.text: is verified to be less-productive land, as compared to other land on the applicable farm.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: B Exclusion
children.text: The term “eligible land” does not include any land that was enrolled in a conservation reserve program contract in any of the 3 crop years preceding enrollment in the pilot program under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a voluntary soil health and income protection pilot program under which eligible land is enrolled through the use of contracts to assist owners and operators of eligible land to conserve and improve the soil, water, and wildlife resources of the eligible land.

children.heading: Deadline for participation
children.label: B Deadline for participation
children.text: Eligible land may be enrolled in the program under this section through .December 31, 20201See Soil Health and Income Protection Pilot Program Extension note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: be entered into by the Secretary, the owner of the eligible land, and (if applicable) the operator of the eligible land; and

children.label: I
children.text: the lowest practicable cost perennial conserving use cover crop for the eligible land, as determined by the applicable State conservationist after considering the advice of the applicable State technical committee, shall be planted on the eligible land;

children.label: II
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (E), the owner or operator of the eligible land shall pay the cost of planting the conserving use cover crop under subclause (I);

children.label: III
children.text: subject to subparagraph (F), the eligible land may be harvested for seed, hayed, or grazed outside the primary nesting season established for the applicable county;

children.label: IV
children.text: the eligible land may be eligible for a walk-in access program of the applicable State, if any; and

children.label: V
children.text: a nonprofit wildlife organization may provide to the owner or operator of the eligible land a payment in exchange for an agreement by the owner or operator not to harvest the conserving use cover.

children.heading: Payments
children.label: B Payments
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (E) and (F)(ii)(II), the annual rental rate for a payment under a contract described in paragraph (2) shall be equal to 50 percent of the average rental rate for the applicable county under , as determined by the Secretary.section 3834(d) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on enrolled land
children.label: C Limitation on enrolled land
children.text: Not more than 15 percent of the eligible land on a farm may be enrolled in the pilot program under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), each contract described in paragraph (2) shall be for a term of 3, 4, or 5 years, as determined by the parties to the contract.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: I Secretary
children.text: The Secretary may terminate a contract described in paragraph (2) before the end of the term described in clause (i) if the Secretary determines that the early termination of the contract is necessary.

children.heading: Owners and operators
children.label: II Owners and operators
children.text: An owner and (if applicable) an operator of eligible land enrolled in the pilot program under this subsection may terminate a contract described in paragraph (2) before the end of the term described in clause (i) if the owner and (if applicable) the operator pay to the Secretary an amount equal to the amount of rental payments received under the contract.

children.heading: Beginning, limited resource, socially disadvantaged, or veteran farmers and ranchers
children.label: E Beginning, limited resource, socially disadvantaged, or veteran farmers and ranchers
I
children.text: using the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall pay 50 percent; and

children.label: II
children.text: the beginning, limited resource, socially disadvantaged, or veteran farmer or rancher shall pay 50 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the annual rental rate for a payment under a contract described in paragraph (2) shall be equal to 75 percent of the average rental rate for the applicable county under , as determined by the Secretary.section 3834(d) of this title

children.heading: Harvesting, haying, and grazing outside applicable period
children.label: F Harvesting, haying, and grazing outside applicable period
i
children.text: with respect to eligible land that is so hayed or grazed, adequate stubble height shall be maintained to protect the soil on the eligible land, as determined by the applicable State conservationist after considering the advice of the applicable State technical committee; and

children.label: I
children.text: the eligible land shall not be eligible to be insured or reinsured under the Federal Crop Insurance Act ( et seq.); and7 U.S.C. 1501

children.label: II
children.text: the rental payment otherwise applicable to the eligible land under this subsection shall be reduced by 25 percent.

children.heading: Acreage limitation
children.label: 4 Acreage limitation
children.text: Of the number of acres available for enrollment in the conservation reserve under , not more than 50,000 total acres of eligible land may be enrolled under the pilot program under this subsection.section 3831(d)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the estimated conservation value of the land; and

children.label: B
children.text: estimated savings from reduced commodity payments, crop insurance indemnities, and crop insurance premium subsidies.
